response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 15+ years of broad full-stack engineering experience, genuine open-source contributions, and solid Linux familiarity that align with several technical requirements of the role. However, two hard disqualifiers stand out: his German is self-described as 'Basic,' well below the C1 minimum required for client-facing consulting work in a German-speaking market, and his career is overwhelmingly that of a senior individual contributor with no demonstrated track record of leading or managing an engineering team. Additional absences — Python, UML expertise, formal project management, and IT security/risk management experience — further widen the gap between his profile and the seniority level this executive-adjacent position demands.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse engineering experience across web, mobile, distributed systems, and embedded work demonstrates rare technical breadth
Active open-source contributor and community educator (workshops, conference talks, GISCollective platform) closely matches the firm's open-source and digital-sovereignty focus
Explicit Linux expertise and experience with DevOps tooling (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab) covers a meaningful subset of the required Linux/Unix infrastructure competency
concerns: German proficiency is self-rated 'Basic,' a critical gap against the mandatory C1 minimum required for direct client advisory and consulting engagements
No evidence of managing or leading a team of developers or consultants; the entire employment history reflects individual-contributor roles with only informal mentoring
Key technical requirements are absent from the resume: Python, UML/modeling expertise, formal requirements management, and IT security or compliance experience

key_factors.factor: No team or project leadership experience
Open-source track record and technical breadth
